patterns.name: Cross-Species Target Organ Concordance
patterns.description: Compares target organs across species. Shared target organ across species is the strongest predictor of human relevance.

confidence_modifiers.note: NHP has high spontaneous background for this organ
Dose ranges do not overlap — non-concordance may reflect dose ceiling, not true species difference

patterns.name: Duration Escalation Trajectory
patterns.description: For the same compound in the same species across increasing study durations — is the finding progressive, stable, or adaptive?

confidence_modifiers.note: Dose levels do not overlap — trajectory comparison not valid

patterns.name: Program NOAEL Reconciliation
patterns.description: Identifies most sensitive species and computes HED for FIH dose selection using FDA BSA scaling.

confidence_modifiers.note: Route differs between studies — dose-based comparison may be misleading without bioavailability data

patterns.name: Monitoring Watchlist
patterns.description: Generates a structured watchlist for ongoing/planned studies based on confirmed and potential target organs from submitted studies.

patterns.name: Novel Finding Flag
patterns.description: Flags syndromes appearing in the current study but absent from all prior studies of the same compound.

confidence_modifiers.note: Prior study used lower doses — finding may appear novel because affected threshold was not reached

patterns.name: Reversibility Comparison
patterns.description: Compares recovery outcomes for the same syndrome/target organ across studies.

patterns.name: Severity Grade Comparison
patterns.description: Ordinal severity comparison for the same syndrome across studies/species.

confidence_modifiers.note: Dose ranges differ — severity comparison at unmatched doses may be misleading
